title: myosotis arvensis subsp. umbrata
intro: Myosotis arvensis subsp. umbrata, also known as wood forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species in the borage family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The wood forget-me-not is also valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and soothing digestive problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to promote relaxation, ease pain, and support overall well-being.
